### 2026-07-08 ‚Äî Audit issue #120 ‚Äî citation coverage across computed quantities (Session 315)
- **Deliverable:** AUDIT_WORKSTREAM report (TDD N/A ‚Äî no code/docs changed): does every computed quantity/statistic/estimator in the package cite its source, consistently, across the parallel doc surfaces (roxygen `@references`, the built vignette manual, `inst/extdata/ui_guidance/*.html`)? Scope gate (owner, one `AskUserQuestion`): broad ‚Äî classify all 50 candidate files, not just the issue's 8 named items. Report: `docs/audits/ISSUE_120_CITATION_COVERAGE_AUDIT_2026-07-08.md`.
- **Coverage:** 50/50 `R/calc*.R` / kinship / demographic / breeding-group-formation files + 8/8 `inst/extdata/ui_guidance/*.html` pages + 8/8 built/orphan vignette `manual_components/*.Rmd` files classified (100%).
- **Findings:** 12 total (5 High, 5 Moderate, 2 Minor). Headline: 5 statistics have **no citation on any doc surface** ‚Äî `calcNeSexRatio` (sex-ratio Ne), `calcGeneDiversity` (GD), `meanKinship`, `rankSubjects` (the core GVA ranking scheme), and `groupAddAssign` (the breeding-group MIS algorithm ‚Äî the namesake algorithm of the package's own Vinson & Raboin 2015 key reference) ‚Äî despite sibling statistics in the same doc panel (`calcNeVariance`, `calcGU`) being correctly and fully cited. Moderate findings: a truncated `@references` on `modBreedingGroups.R`; the Lacy (1989) citation present only inside `@examples` comments (not a formal `@references` field) for `calcFE`/`calcFG`/`calcFEFG`/`calcRetention`; two live, built manual pages (`_breeding_group_algorithm.Rmd`, `_genome_uniqueness_algorithm.Rmd`) describing algorithms with zero citations even though a third page (`gvAndBgDesc.html`) correctly cites both; `population_genetics_terms.html` doesn't define Genome Uniqueness or Mean Kinship at all; `calcGU`/`calcA` missing the MacCluer et al. (1986) co-citation `gvAndBgDesc.html` gives the same gene-drop algorithm. Minor: a Lange 1997-vs-2002 edition mismatch across two doc surfaces plus a self-flagged-stale URL in `kinship.R`; two orphan duplicate vignette source files not wired into the built manual.
- **No fixes applied this session** (audit and fix are separate sessions per AUDIT_WORKSTREAM and the #109 precedent) ‚Äî issue **#120 left open**; report ¬ß6 gives a priority-ordered, per-finding fix list for a follow-on session.
- **Files:** `docs/audits/ISSUE_120_CITATION_COVERAGE_AUDIT_2026-07-08.md` (new). No `R/`, `vignettes/`, or `inst/` files changed.

### 2026-07-08 ‚Äî Fix issue #121 ‚Äî test suite now WARN 0; guard `getPedMaxAge()` empty/all-NA input (Session 314)
- **Deliverable:** Eliminate the 7 unasserted test warnings so the suite runs `WARN 0` (keeping `FAIL 0`), under strict TDD (PRE-RED ‚Üí RED ‚Üí GREEN ‚Üí concluded no-refactor). 1 scope/approach `AskUserQuestion` + 3 phase-gate `AskUserQuestion`s; 0 stakeholder corrections.
- **Root cause (modPyramid, 5 warnings):** `getPedMaxAge()` (`R/getPedMaxAge.R:25`) ran `max(ped$age, na.rm = TRUE)` on a pedigree with no `age` column or all-NA ages, returning `-Inf` and emitting `no non-missing arguments to max; returning -Inf`. The pyramid module's `renderPlot` re-fired on each `setInputs`, leaking 5 copies. Runtime-reachable (a colony import lacking birth dates), risking a `-Inf` axis bound ‚Äî so fixed at the source, not the test.
- **GREEN:** `getPedMaxAge()` now returns `NA_real_` when there are no non-missing ages (its sole caller, `getPyramidPlot.R:52`, already maps `NA` ‚Üí `binWidth`); `@return` roxygen updated and `man/getPedMaxAge.Rd` regenerated.
- **gvaConvergence (2 warnings):** the deliberately-invalid PSD-bound override path emits the `checkKinshipOverrides` ">0.5" diagnostic twice (via `prepareKinshipOverrides` + `applyKinshipOverrides`) before erroring; the test asserted the error but leaked the warnings. Tightened `test_gvaConvergence_kinshipOverrides.R:157` to `expect_warning(expect_error(...))`, asserting both (test-only hygiene; no source change).
- **RED:** new `test_getPedMaxAge.R` block asserting `NA` + no warning on no-age-column and all-NA-age input (4 failures under the old source).
- **Verification:** full suite `FAIL 0 ¬∑ ERROR 0 ¬∑ WARN 0` (3740 passed, 167 skipped); `devtools::check()` 0/0/0; lint 0 on `R/getPedMaxAge.R`; Phase-3E runtime probe rendered `getPyramidPlot()` on all-NA / no-age / real-age pedigrees with 0 warnings.
- **Files:** `R/getPedMaxAge.R`, `man/getPedMaxAge.Rd`, `tests/testthat/test_getPedMaxAge.R`, `tests/testthat/test_gvaConvergence_kinshipOverrides.R`, `NEWS.Rmd`, `NEWS.md`. Closes #121.

### 2026-07-08 ‚Äî Implement issue #118 Slice 4 ‚Äî publish / user documentation; **closes #118** (Session 313)
- **Deliverable:** Slice 4 (the final slice) of the #118 plan (`docs/planning/issue118-effective-population-size-plan.md` ¬ß8) under strict TDD (PRE-RED ‚Üí RED ‚Üí GREEN ‚Üí concluded no-refactor). 4 `AskUserQuestion` gates (surface scope, PRE-RED‚ÜíRED, RED‚ÜíGREEN, GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR); 0 stakeholder corrections. Plain-language user documentation for the three effective-population-size metrics (gene diversity, sex-ratio Ne, variance Ne) *where they display*. **This slice closes #118** (E1‚ÄìE3 scope; E4 rate-of-coancestry remains deferred to a future effort, plan ¬ß11).
- **RED (strict TDD, docs-as-tests):** new `tests/testthat/test_effectivePopulationSizeDocs.R` ‚Äî a static source-scanning guard mirroring `test_kinshipOverrideDocs.R` (the S285 precedent): `system.file()` + `readLines()` + `grepl()` asserting each metric is defined/explained in the surface where it renders. RED = 19 phrase-absent failures with the file-found checks passing (structurally sound).
- **GREEN (documentation, house style):** `inst/extdata/ui_guidance/population_genetics_terms.html` ‚Äî three new definition blocks (mirroring the Founder Equivalents / Lacy blocks): Gene Diversity (`GD = 1 ‚àí 1/(2¬∑FG)`, expected heterozygosity retained), Sex-Ratio effective size (`4¬∑Nm¬∑Nf/(Nm+Nf)`, over current living breeders, balanced-vs-harem, 0 when a sex is absent), Variance effective size (the general Crow & Kimura 1970 form, over living breeders of all sexes, N/A when fewer than two), a shared Wright-Fisher idealization caveat, and a plain-language rate-of-coancestry forward-pointer. `summary_stats.html` ‚Äî a note on the GD column and the "Effective Population Size" block over *current living breeders* vs the analysis set. `genetic_value.html` ‚Äî a cross-reference to the definitions panel. `vignettes/manual_components/_summary_statistics.Rmd` ‚Äî the three metrics listed with their assumptions (rendered via `a3manual.Rmd`).
- **NEWS (source-of-truth fix):** added the user-facing bullet to `NEWS.Rmd` (the human-authored, build-ignored source) and rendered `NEWS.md` from it. A trial render surfaced a pre-existing divergence ‚Äî S307 had hand-added the `minSireAge`/`minDamAge` (#119) bullet to `NEWS.md` only ‚Äî so backfilled #119 into `NEWS.Rmd` before rendering, leaving `NEWS.md` a faithful render with nothing dropped. (Owner directive: edit `NEWS.Rmd`, not `NEWS.md`.)
- **Verification:** new guard **23 pass / 0 fail**; full suite **3735 pass / 0 fail / 0 error / 167 skip** (0 true offenders excl. app/e2e noise); spelling clean (no WORDLIST change needed ‚Äî "coancestry" lives only in the non-scanned HTML); `devtools::check()` **0/0/0** (builds the vignettes). Phase 3E: rendered both module UIs and confirmed the guidance HTML is embedded at runtime (`includeHTML(system.file(...))`), so the running app serves the new text.
- **Also (close-out, owner-directed):** filed issue **#121** ‚Äî the suite is `FAIL 0` but emits 7 unasserted warnings (`test_modPyramid.R` √ó5: `max()` on an empty vector ‚Üí `-Inf` during a reactive re-render; `test_gvaConvergence_kinshipOverrides.R` √ó2: the deliberately-invalid PSD-bound override path). Pre-existing test-hygiene, unrelated to this slice; owner deferred it to a later session. Also noted for my own routine: the close-out "clean regression read" should inspect the `warning` column, not just `failed`/`error`/`skipped`.

### 2026-07-08 ‚Äî Implement issue #118 Slice 3 ‚Äî E3 variance effective size (`calcNeVariance`) (Session 312)
- **Deliverable:** Slice 3 of the #118 plan (`docs/planning/issue118-effective-population-size-plan.md` ¬ß8) under strict TDD (PRE-RED ‚Üí RED ‚Üí GREEN ‚Üí concluded no-refactor). 5 `AskUserQuestion` gates (pre-RED scope D-d formula + N<2 degeneracy, then PRE-RED‚ÜíRED, RED‚ÜíGREEN, GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR); 0 stakeholder corrections. ONE slice ‚Äî Slice 4 (publish/docs) not started.
- **Load-bearing owner decision (D-d), and a plan correction:** the plan (Dragon D-7 / D-d) recommended the "mean-adjusted Crow-Kimura form" *on the rationale that the bare `(4N-4)/(Vk+2)` "overstates" Ne*. Computing all candidates on the real bundled pedigree (N=156, kÃÑ=5.28, Vk=16.54) showed that rationale is **inverted**: the bare form gives the **lowest** value (33.4), the mean-adjusted general form gives **110.9**, and a rescale-to-replacement variant gives 141.7. The plan's headline "skew drags E3 far below E2" only holds under the dimensionally-inconsistent bare form (which mixes a kÃÑ=5.28-scale variance into a kÃÑ=2 formula). Presented the three candidates with real values and the correction; **owner ratified the general Crow-Kimura form** `Ne = (N¬∑kÃÑ ‚àí 1)/(kÃÑ ‚àí 1 + Vk/kÃÑ)` (the standard generalization; reduces to the classic `(4N‚àí2)/(Vk+2)` at replacement) and **N<2 ‚Üí NA**.
- **New (`R/calcNeVariance.R`, exported):** `calcNeVariance(ped)` over the current living breeders ‚Äî reuses the S311 `getLivingBreeders(ped)` helper (N = number of living breeders; kÃÑ, Vk from `findOffspring()` lifetime offspring counts, all sexes counted); `NA` when fewer than 2 living breeders (variance undefined). Roxygen states the formula, the living-breeder population, the Wright-Fisher idealizing assumptions, and `@references Crow & Kimura (1970)`. `1.0` house-style literals (implicit_integer_linter).
- **Wired additively:** `reportGV()` bundle gains the colony-level scalar `neVariance` (after `neSexRatio`) + `@return` clause; threaded through the `founderStats` reactive into a SECOND "Variance Ne" column in the existing `modSummaryStats` "Effective Population Size" block (not a third block) and a `Variance Ne (living breeders)` row in the `modGeneticValue` GV-tab summary. `fe`/`fg`/`fgSE`/`neGD`/`neSexRatio`/founder-count golden-master unchanged.
- **Verification:** full suite **3712 pass / 0 fail / 0 error** (touched files 0 skipped under `NOT_CRAN=true`); lint **0** on all 4 changed R files; spelling clean (`Kimura` hand-added to `inst/WORDLIST` sorted ‚Äî `update_wordlist` NOT run); `devtools::check()` **0/0/0**; `man/calcNeVariance.Rd` + `NAMESPACE` `export(calcNeVariance)` regenerated. Phase 3E on real `qcPed`: 18 living breeders ‚Üí `neVariance = 26.41` rendered live beside `neSexRatio = 17.78` in the Effective Population Size block. (Note: on qcPed Ne_v 26.41 > E2 17.78 > census 18 ‚Äî families are more even than Poisson, so the variance Ne legitimately exceeds the census.)
- **Also (unrelated, owner-requested):** filed issue **#120** ‚Äî an AUDIT_WORKSTREAM task for a future session: "are there reference citations for each calculation in the package?"

### 2026-07-08 ‚Äî Implement issue #118 Slice 2 ‚Äî E2 demographic sex-ratio Ne (`calcNeSexRatio`) (Session 311)
- **Deliverable:** Slice 2 of the #118 plan (`docs/planning/issue118-effective-population-size-plan.md` ¬ß8) under strict TDD (PRE-RED ‚Üí RED ‚Üí GREEN ‚Üí concluded no-refactor). 4 `AskUserQuestion` gates (pre-RED scope D-b/D-c/D-a + PRE-RED‚ÜíRED + RED‚ÜíGREEN + GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR); 0 stakeholder corrections. ONE slice ‚Äî E3 (Slice 3) not started.
- **Owner scope decisions (this session, via `AskUserQuestion`):** **D-b ‚Äî living breeders in the whole pedigree** (living animals appearing as a sire/dam anywhere in `ped`, U-ids excluded, all-living when no `exit` column), independent of the proband/population selection. **D-c ‚Äî `Ne_sr = 0`** when one breeding sex is absent (`Nm==0` or `Nf==0`, including no living breeders at all). **D-a (E2) ‚Äî a separate "Effective Population Size" block** labeled "current living breeders"; Gene Diversity (GD) stays beside FG (the separate block deferred from Slice 1 is introduced here, the first living-breeder metric).
- **New functions:** `getLivingBreeders(ped)` (`@noRd` shared helper, reused by Slice 3/E3) returns the ids of living breeders; `calcNeSexRatio(ped)` (`@export`) = `4¬∑Nm¬∑Nf/(Nm+Nf)` over living breeders with known sex, `0` when a breeding sex is absent. `reportGV()` bundle gains the colony-level scalar `neSexRatio` (additively, after `neGD`); threaded through the `founderStats` reactive into a separate labeled block in `modSummaryStats` and a `Sex-Ratio Ne (living breeders)` row in the `modGeneticValue` GV-tab summary.
- **Verification:** full suite pass / 0 fail / 0 error (touched files 0 skipped under `NOT_CRAN=true`); `fe`/`fg`/`fgSE`/`neGD`/founder-count golden-master unchanged; lint 0 on changed files; spelling clean; `devtools::check()` 0/0/0. Phase 3E on real `qcPed` data: 18 living breeders (Nm=8, Nf=10) ‚Üí `neSexRatio = 17.78`, rendered in both live surfaces.

### 2026-07-08 ‚Äî Implement issue #118 Slice 1 ‚Äî E1 gene diversity (`calcGeneDiversity`) (Session 310)
- **Deliverable:** Slice 1 (the tracer bullet) of the #118 plan (`docs/planning/issue118-effective-population-size-plan.md` ¬ß8) under strict TDD (PRE-RED ‚Üí RED ‚Üí GREEN ‚Üí concluded no-refactor). 4 `AskUserQuestion` gates (pre-RED scope D-a/D-e/D-f + PRE-RED‚ÜíRED + RED‚ÜíGREEN + GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR); 0 stakeholder corrections. ONE slice ‚Äî E2/E3 (Slices 2/3) not started.
- **Owner scope decisions (this session, via `AskUserQuestion`):** **D-a ‚Äî GD displays *beside FG*** (not in a separate "Effective Population Size" block): firsthand, GD is over the SAME population as FG (the analysis set), unlike E2/E3 which are over living breeders, so the Dragon D-1 population-conflation concern that motivated the separate block does not apply to E1; the separate block is deferred to Slice 2 (E2), the first living-breeder metric. **D-e ‚Äî `calcGeneDiversity(fg)`** exported, bundle field `neGD`. **D-f ‚Äî `gdSE` deferred** (a trivial function of the existing `fgSE`; addable later without rework).
- **New (`R/calcGeneDiversity.R`, exported):** `calcGeneDiversity(fg) = 1 - 1/(2*fg)` ‚Äî gene diversity (expected heterozygosity retained relative to the founding gene pool), a pure deterministic function of the founder genome equivalents `FG`. `NA` propagates from a degenerate (`NA`) `FG`. Mirrors the `calcFE`/`calcFG`/`calcFGSE` idiom. `1L`/`2L` house-style literals (implicit_integer_linter).
- **Wired additively (`R/reportGV.R`):** `neGD = calcGeneDiversity(feFg$FG)` added to the `nprcgenekeeprGV` bundle after `fgSE` (before `maleFounders`) + documented in `@return`. A colony-level scalar like `fgSE` ‚Äî NOT a per-animal `$report`/`$gu` column. Golden-master: `fe`/`fg`/`fgSE`/founder counts and the `$report` columns unchanged (the bundled-report `fg=52.75` tests still pass).
- **Display (both live surfaces):** `R/modGeneticValue.R` ‚Äî `founderStats` reactive now threads `neGD = fr$neGD`; `gvSummary` appends a "Gene Diversity (GD)" Metric/Value row (`%.4f`, N/A-guarded for older results / degenerate `FG`). `R/modSummaryStats.R` ‚Äî the Summary-Statistics founder table gains a "Gene Diversity (GD)" `<th>` + `<td>` (`%.4f`, same guard), mirroring the existing `FG +/- SE` inline pattern. `makeFounderStatsTable()` (dead helper, no runtime caller) intentionally NOT touched ‚Äî not in the plan's Slice-1 GREEN; it silently ignores the extra `neGD` field, so no breakage.
- **Tests (strict TDD, all RED-verified failing for the right reason first):** new `tests/testthat/test_calcGeneDiversity.R` (exact `calcGeneDiversity(20)==0.975`, `(0.5)==0`, `(1)==0.5`, NA-propagation, monotonicity, scalar type); extended `test_reportGV.R` (both `expect_named` bundle assertions gain `neGD`; new test `neGD == 1-1/(2*fg)`, scalar, in `(0,1)`, not a per-animal column); extended `test_modSummaryStats.R` (founder table renders the GD header + value `0.9905`); extended `test_modGeneticValue.R` (`gvSummary` shows a Gene Diversity row).
- **Verify:** full suite **3657 pass / 0 fail / 0 error / 0 true offenders** (run with `NOT_CRAN=true` so the `skip_on_cran` module test files actually execute); lint **0** real findings on all 4 changed R files; `spelling::spell_check_package` **clean** (`heterozygosity` hand-added to `inst/WORDLIST` in sorted position ‚Äî `update_wordlist` NOT run); `devtools::check()` **0 ERROR / 0 WARNING / 0 NOTE**; `man/calcGeneDiversity.Rd` + `NAMESPACE` `export(calcGeneDiversity)` regenerated.
- **Phase 3E (runtime behavior changed ‚Äî display):** drove the real end-to-end render on **real qcPed data** ‚Äî `reportGV(qcPed)` ‚Üí `neGD=0.990528` ‚Üí threaded through `founderStats` ‚Üí `modSummaryStatsServer` renders the founder table `Known Founders 81 | Female 61 | Male 20 | FE 77.04 | FG 52.79 +/- 0.04 | Gene Diversity (GD) 0.9905`. GD renders beside FG with the actual computed value, not just the test fixture.

### 2026-07-07 ‚Äî Plan issue #118 ‚Äî effective population size (Ne) estimates (Session 309)
- **Deliverable:** Planning document `docs/planning/issue118-effective-population-size-plan.md` (planning session ‚Äî the plan is the deliverable; implementation is separate later sessions, one estimator each, per FM #18). Seed: the S308 triage.
- **Owner decisions (gathered this session via `AskUserQuestion`):** implement **E1** (gene diversity `GD = 1 - 1/(2¬∑FG)`) + **E2** (demographic sex-ratio `4¬∑Nm¬∑Nf/(Nm+Nf)`) + **E3** (variance effective size) as a labeled multi-metric set over **current living breeders**, displayed in the GVA founder-stats surface. **E4** (rate-of-coancestry) **deferred**; its future-work avenues (generation length computed-from-data + user slider; cross-generation coancestry-rate estimate) posted as a comment on #118.
- **Plan built from firsthand evidence** (read `reportGV.R`, `makeFounderStatsTable.R`, `findOffspring.R`, `offspringCounts.R`, `modGeneticValue.R`, `modSummaryStats.R`; an Explore wiring/test map; and a worked numeric Ne on the bundled example pedigree: living breeders `Nm=35`, `Nf=121` ‚Üí **E2=108.6**; `N=156`, `Vk=16.5` ‚Üí **E3=33.5** ‚Äî skew drags Ne ~3√ó below the sex-ratio figure). 4 implementation slices (E1 ‚Üí E2 ‚Üí E3 ‚Üí publish/docs) with per-slice completion criteria + 8 consolidated dragons.
- **Corrections to the triage (firsthand):** (1) the triage's stated display home `makeFounderStatsTable()`/`modFounderStats` is a **dead helper** ‚Äî `makeFounderStatsTable()` has no runtime caller and `modFounderStats.R` does not exist; the live surface is `modSummaryStats.R:623-651` + `modGeneticValue.R:394-478`, fed from the `reportGV.R:264-276` bundle. (2) `sex` is a factor `{F,M,H,U}` (`NA‚Üí"U"`), so E2 must count only `sex=="M"`/`sex=="F"`. (3) `kbar‚âà5.3‚âÝ2` on the real colony, so E3 needs the mean-adjusted variance form, not the bare `(4N-4)/(Vk+2)`.
- **No code changed** (planning deliverable): Phase-3E runtime smoke N/A; package build/spelling unaffected (`docs/` is not part of the R-package build). Close-out: this entry + PROJECT_LEARNINGS 287 + handoff.

### 2026-07-07 ‚Äî Triage issue #118 ‚Äî "Add the effective population size estimate" (Session 308)
- **Deliverable:** Triage / scoping of #118 (analysis only ‚Äî no code changed): `docs/audits/ISSUE_118_EFFECTIVE_POPULATION_SIZE_TRIAGE_2026-07-07.md` + a condensed comment on #118. Close-out: this CHANGELOG entry + PROJECT_LEARNINGS 286 + handoff.
- **Verdict:** A well-motivated **enhancement** (not a bug, not blocked), but **under-specified in two ways**: (1) "effective population size" is a *family* of estimators (E1 founder-genome-equivalent/gene-diversity, E2 demographic sex-ratio `4NmNf/(Nm+Nf)`, E3 variance/reproductive-skew, E4 rate-of-coancestry `1/(2¬∑dCbar)`), each answering a different question and needing different inputs; (2) the issue itself notes "place to locate output has not been determined."
- **Evidence-based inventory:** most inputs already exist ‚Äî FG + its SE (`calcFG`/`calcFGSE`), FE (`calcFE`), founder sex counts (`reportGV`), per-animal offspring counts (`findOffspring`, for E3's Vk), mean kinship (`meanKinship`), gene-drop retention (`geneDrop`/`calcRetention`), generation *numbers* (`findGeneration`). **Gap:** no generation *length* in years / no longitudinal baseline, so E4 needs new data or a scoped assumption. Natural output home: the GVA founder-stats table (`makeFounderStatsTable`/`modFounderStats`/the `reportGV` bundle), where Ne sits beside FE/FG as another colony-level scalar ‚Äî directly answering the issue's open "location" question.
- **Recommendation (direction, not decision):** owner picks the estimator(s) and population in a short planning/`/grill-me` session, then a `docs/planning/issue118-*-plan.md` with per-slice completion criteria drives one-estimator-per-session TDD slices. The genetics-management choice (which Ne, which population) is the owner's. **No implementation this session.**

### 2026-07-07 ‚Äî Implement issue #119 Slice 4 ‚Äî Shiny two-field sire/dam age UX + `modPotentialParents`/`appServer` wiring (Session 306)
- **Deliverable:** Slice 4 of the #119 plan (`docs/planning/issue119-sex-specific-min-breeding-age-plan.md` ¬ß3 Slice 4) under strict TDD (PRE-RED ‚Üí RED ‚Üí GREEN ‚Üí concluded no-refactor). 4 `AskUserQuestion` gates (R2 ratification + PRE-RED‚ÜíRED + RED‚ÜíGREEN + GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR); 0 stakeholder corrections. (2) **Potential Parents follows the Input-tab fields** (one source of truth). (3) **Sire/Dam labels.**
- **Changed (`R/modInput.R`):** the single `textInput("minParentAge")` (prefilled `"2.0"`) becomes two optional blank fields, `minSireAge` ("Minimum Sire Age (years)") and `minDamAge` ("Minimum Dam Age (years)"), with help text "Leave blank to use the species-specific default for each sex". New `@noRd` helper `parseOptionalAge()` turns a field value into a numeric floor or `NULL` (blank / whitespace / `NA` / non-numeric ‚Üí `NULL`). The `getData` observer now threads `parseOptionalAge(input$minSireAge)`/`parseOptionalAge(input$minDamAge)` into `qcStudbook`/`runQcStudbook` (blank ‚Üí `NULL` ‚Üí the species+sex table default). The single exposed `minParentAge` reactive is replaced by `minSireAge`/`minDamAge` reactives (return list grows 9 ‚Üí 10; the retired reactive had **no consumer** in `appServer.R`).
- **Changed (`R/modPotentialParents.R`):** `modPotentialParentsServer()` signature `minParentAge = 2.0` ‚Üí `minSireAge = NULL, minDamAge = NULL`; each floor is normalized with `if (is.function(x)) x() else x` so it accepts either a reactive (wired from the Input tab) or a plain scalar/`NULL`, then forwarded to `getPotentialParents`. `NULL` ‚Üí the species+sex table default.
- **Wired (`R/appServer.R:345`):** `modPotentialParentsServer` is now passed `minSireAge = inputResults$minSireAge`, `minDamAge = inputResults$minDamAge` (previously invoked with no age argument ‚Üí a hardcoded 2.0, unwired to the Input tab). QC and Potential Parents now honor the same user-set floors.
- **Tests:** two new files ‚Äî `test_modInput_sexSpecificAge.R` (parseOptionalAge unit; two-field UI render + old field retired; the two reactives; blank‚ÜíNULL / typed‚Üínumeric / invalid‚ÜíNULL; getData threads the floors into the QC callees via mock-capture) and `test_modPotentialParents_sexSpecificAge.R` (module forwards scalar AND reactive floors, defaults to NULL, and honors an impossibly-high sire floor end-to-end). Migrated the retired single-field API in `test_modInput.R`, `test_modInput_coverage.R`, `test_modInput_qcStudbook.R`, `test_modInput_incomplete_final_line.R`, `test_modPotentialParents(_coverage).R`, `stubInput` in `test_appServer_server.R`, and **five `test-e2e-*` files** (the app-driver layer referenced the old `dataInput-minParentAge` input id and the "Minimum Parent Age" label). Deprecated-alias `runQcStudbook(minParentAge=)` calls (R-level, Slices 1‚Äì3 scope) intentionally kept.
- **Verify:** full suite 3632 pass / 0 fail / 0 error / 0 true offenders (7 warnings are the pre-existing `test_gvaConvergence`/`test_modPyramid` baseline); `devtools::check(--as-cran)` **0 ERROR / 0 WARNING / 0 NOTE**; lint 0 on all 3 changed R files; `man/modInputServer.Rd` + `man/modPotentialParentsServer.Rd` regenerated (NAMESPACE unchanged).

### 2026-07-07 ‚Äî Implement issue #119 Slice 3 ‚Äî `minDamAge` in `getProductionStatus` (Session 305)
- **Deliverable:** Slice 3 of the #119 plan (`docs/planning/issue119-sex-specific-min-breeding-age-plan.md` ¬ß3 Slice 3) under strict TDD (PRE-RED ‚Üí RED ‚Üí GREEN ‚Üí concluded no-refactor). 4 `AskUserQuestion` gates (R1 ratification + PRE-RED‚ÜíRED + RED‚ÜíGREEN + GREEN‚ÜíREFACTOR); 0 stakeholder corrections. ONE slice ‚Äî Slices 4‚Äì5 not started.
- **R1 ratified (owner): preserve 3.** The production-status dam floor of **3** is kept as a distinct demographic threshold (breeding-capacity females for the Production = Births/Dams ratio) ‚Äî NOT unified to the species+sex breeding-age table (which would drop it to 2 / 2.5 and change every production ratio). `getProductionStatus`' dam floor keeps `default = 3`; the caller keeps passing `3L`. Every production golden is unchanged.
- **Changed:** `getProductionStatus()` (internal, `@noRd`) renames its scalar `minParentAge` parameter to `minDamAge` (default `3L`); `minParentAge` becomes a **deprecated alias** (`lifecycle::deprecate_warn(when = "2.0.0")`) that sets `minDamAge`. The dam-count filter is now `sex == "F" & age >= minDamAge`. Its only caller `getGeneticDiversityStats()` migrates its internal call from `minParentAge = 3L` to `minDamAge = 3L` (production output identical). No `minSireAge` param ‚Äî production status is females-only (plan Dragon).
- **Tests:** `test_getProductionStatus.R` ‚Äî 11 golden calls migrated to `minDamAge` (counts byte-identical), plus a new override test (`minDamAge = 100` ‚Üí 0 dams ‚Üí NA / green, proving the parameter drives the filter) and a deprecation-path test (`minParentAge = 3.0` still reproduces the floor-3 result and warns via `lifecycle::expect_deprecated`).
- **Verification:** full suite 2944 pass / 0 fail / 0 error / 0 true offenders; `devtools::check(--as-cran)` 0 ERROR / 0 WARNING / 0 NOTE; lint 0 on both changed R files; `document()` produced no man/NAMESPACE diff (`@noRd`; the lifecycle imports already existed). No man page (the function is `@noRd`); no runtime app change ‚Äî the change is behavior-preserving and its runtime path (`modGeneticDiversity` ‚Üí `getGeneticDiversityStats` ‚Üí `getProductionStatus`) is covered by the passing `test_modGeneticDiversity` / `test_getGeneticDiversityStats` tests.

### 2026-07-07 ‚Äî Implement issue #119 Slice 2 ‚Äî sex-specific `minSireAge`/`minDamAge` in `getPotentialParents` (Session 304)
- **Deliverable:** Slice 2 of the #119 plan (`docs/planning/issue119-sex-specific-min-breeding-age-plan.md` ¬ß3 Slice 2) under strict TDD (PRE-RED ‚Üí RED ‚Üí GREEN ‚Üí concluded no-refactor). 3 `AskUserQuestion` gates; 0 stakeholder corrections. ONE slice ‚Äî Slices 3‚Äì5 not started.
- **Changed:** `getPotentialParents()` gains `minSireAge`/`minDamAge` (default `NULL` ‚Üí per-candidate species+sex floor via the Slice-1 `resolveBreedingAge()` helper), replacing the single flat age cutoff. Each candidate is now judged against its OWN species+sex floor before the sire/dam split, so a 3-yr rhesus male is not proposed as a sire (floor 4) while species-less pedigrees reproduce the flat-2 behavior exactly. `minParentAge` becomes a **deprecated alias** (`lifecycle::deprecate_warn(when = "2.0.0")`) that sets both new params; `minParentAge = NULL` still disables the age gate (legacy skip, mapped to `-Inf` floors). Gestation logic (`maxGestationalPeriod`, dam-exclusion window) untouched.
- **Internal caller:** `modPotentialParentsServer` (`R/modPotentialParents.R:263`) rewired to pass its `minParentAge` through the new `minSireAge`/`minDamAge` params so the app never trips `getPotentialParents`'s own deprecation warning (mirrors Slice 1's `modInput` rewire; the two-field UX stays Slice 4).
- **Back-compat:** species-less `rhesusPedigree` goldens unchanged ‚Äî the 13 existing `minParentAge=` test calls migrated to explicit `minSireAge = 2, minDamAge = 2` (numerically identical, since an explicit override wins over the table even when species is present); only species-aware assertions were added (sire/dam exclusion + per-sex overrides + deprecation path). The Slice-1 `tests/testthat/setup.R` (`lifecycle_verbosity = "quiet"`) already covers Slice 2's new deprecation warnings.
- **Verify:** full suite 2939 pass / 0 fail / 0 error / 0 true offenders; `devtools::check(--as-cran)` 0 ERROR / 0 WARNING / 0 NOTE (vignettes rebuilt, no deprecation tripped); lint 0 on changed files; `man/getPotentialParents.Rd` regenerated. Phase-3E: the module rewire is behavior-preserving and exercised by 87 passing `test_modPotentialParents*` server tests (the interactive app smoke belongs to Slice 4's actual UX change).
- **Deferred (per plan):** Slice 3 `getProductionStatus` (+ RATIFY R1 the 2-vs-3 default); Slice 4 Shiny two-field UX (mandatory interactive smoke) & `modPotentialParents`/`appServer` wiring; Slice 5 docs/vignettes/NEWS/WORDLIST. `inst/extdata/trulyUnknownParents.R:23` still uses `minParentAge = 2L` (archived example script ‚Äî not package runtime or a vignette, so it never trips the build; left for Slice 5 or as archived).

### 2026-07-07 ‚Äî Implement issue #119 Slice 1 ‚Äî QC vertical: sex-specific `minSireAge`/`minDamAge` in `checkParentAge`/`qcStudbook`/`runQcStudbook` (Session 303)
- **Deliverable:** Slice 1 of the #119 plan (`docs/planning/issue119-sex-specific-min-breeding-age-plan.md` ¬ß3) under strict TDD (PRE-RED ‚Üí RED ‚Üí GREEN ‚Üí concluded no-refactor). 4 `AskUserQuestion` gates; 0 stakeholder corrections. ONE slice ‚Äî Slices 2‚Äì5 not started.
- **Added:** internal `resolveBreedingAge()` (`R/resolveBreedingAge.R`, `@noRd`) wraps the tested `getSpeciesMinBreedingAge()` ‚Äî both overrides `NULL` ‚Üí per-(species, sex) table floor; an explicit `minSireAge`/`minDamAge` overrides that sex. New `tests/testthat/test_resolveBreedingAge.R` (15 assertions).
- **Changed:** `checkParentAge()`, `qcStudbook()`, `runQcStudbook()` gain `minSireAge`/`minDamAge` (default `NULL` ‚Üí species+sex table lookup, keyed on the **parent's** species via a sire/dam species map built before the merges). `checkParentAge` now applies per-parent, sex- and species-specific floors instead of one flat cutoff ‚Äî resolving #119. `minParentAge` becomes a **deprecated alias** (`lifecycle::deprecate_warn(when = "2.0.0")`) that sets both new params; `minParentAge = NULL` still disables the check (legacy skip preserved exactly, mapped to `-Inf` floors). Internal Shiny caller `modInput.R` (2 QC call sites) rewired to the new params (single UI field feeds both ‚Äî the two-field UX is Slice 4) so the app never trips its own deprecation warning (which `modInput`'s `tryCatch(warning=)` would swallow into an empty errorLst).
- **Back-compat:** species-less fixtures (`qcPed`, `pedGood`, ‚Ä¶) behave exactly as before (table lookup falls back to floor 2); every existing golden count is unchanged ‚Äî only species-aware assertions were added. New `tests/testthat/setup.R` sets `options(lifecycle_verbosity = "quiet")` so the package's own test suite is deterministically clean while external callers still see the deprecation (deprecation-asserting tests force `"warning"` locally).
- **Verify:** full suite 2929 pass / 0 fail / 0 error; `R CMD check --as-cran` 0 ERROR / 0 WARNING / 1 baseline NOTE (archived-on-CRAN); vignettes rebuild OK; lint 0 on changed files; `man/*.Rd` regenerated; Phase-3E runtime smoke of the rewired QC calls PASS.
- **Deferred (per plan):** Slice 2 `getPotentialParents`; Slice 3 `getProductionStatus` (+ RATIFY R1 2-vs-3); Slice 4 Shiny two-field UX (mandatory interactive smoke) & `modPotentialParents`/`appServer` wiring; Slice 5 docs/vignettes/NEWS/WORDLIST.

### 2026-07-07 ‚Äî Plan issue #119 ‚Äî replace scalar `minParentAge` with sex-specific `minSireAge`/`minDamAge` (Session 302)
- **Deliverable:** implementation plan `docs/planning/issue119-sex-specific-min-breeding-age-plan.md` (multi-slice, strict TDD). **Planning only; no code changed** ‚Äî the plan is the deliverable, implementation is separate one-slice-per-session work. Two `AskUserQuestion` ratifications with the owner; 0 stakeholder corrections. Seeded by the S301 triage (`docs/audits/ISSUE_119_MINPARENTAGE_TRIAGE_2026-07-07.md`).
- **Ratified direction (owner, S302):** **(D1) table-backed sex scalars** ‚Äî `minSireAge`/`minDamAge` default to `NULL`, meaning "look up the floor per *parent* species+sex via `getSpeciesMinBreedingAge()`"; an explicit number overrides that sex's floor; absent/unknown species falls back to `2` (unchanged behavior for species-less data). Resolves BOTH halves of #119 (sex AND species) with one source of truth ‚Äî no third parallel notion of breeding age. **(D2) back-compat split by surface** ‚Äî keep `minParentAge` as a deprecated alias (sets both, warns) in the exported R signatures so external scripts don't break, but fully migrate the package-internal Shiny UI to `minSireAge`/`minDamAge` now.
- **Plan shape:** one shared internal resolver (`resolveBreedingAge`, wrapping the existing tested `getSpeciesMinBreedingAge`) + `lifecycle::deprecate_warn` (pattern mirrors `runModularApp.R:25`). Five vertical slices, one session each: **S1** QC vertical (`checkParentAge` + `qcStudbook` + `runQcStudbook` + resolver), **S2** `getPotentialParents`, **S3** `getProductionStatus` + `getGeneticDiversityStats` caller, **S4** Shiny UI migration (`modInput`/`modPotentialParents`/`appServer` wiring ‚Äî runtime Phase-3E mandatory), **S5** docs/vignettes/screenshot/WORDLIST/NEWS. Each slice preserves species-less goldens exactly and only *adds* species-aware assertions.
- **Evidence-based inventory (mandatory for signature-change plans):** firsthand `git grep` over `R/ tests/ man/ vignettes/ inst/` ‚Äî 3 decision sites (`checkParentAge.R:94-95`, `getPotentialParents.R:97`, `getProductionStatus.R:64`), the QC plumbing (`qcStudbook`/`runQcStudbook`), the Shiny surface (`modInput.R:127,448-472,659`, `modPotentialParents.R:223,264`, `appServer.R:345`), plus all tests/man/vignette references, tabulated in the plan.
- **Deferred RATIFY points** (settled during their named slice): the 2-vs-3 `getProductionStatus` default (recommend preserve 3 as an explicit dam override ‚Äî don't silently unify), the two-field Shiny UX, and the deprecation version string. **Load-bearing finds:** `checkParentAge` floor must key on the *parent's* species (add a sire/dam species merge, mirroring `sireBirth`/`damBirth`); `modPotentialParentsServer` at `appServer.R:345` is invoked WITHOUT `minParentAge` (unwired to the Input field) and the `minParentAge` reactive `modInput.R:659` exposes has NO consumer in `appServer.R` ‚Äî a latent loose thread the UI slice resolves.

### 2026-07-07 ‚Äî Triage issue #119 ‚Äî `minParentAge` vs. sex-specific breeding ages (Session 301)
- **Deliverable:** triage/scope GitHub issue **#119** ("Use of `minParentAge` seems to conflict with newer sex specific minimum reproductive ages") ‚Äî the issue had no body. **Analysis only; no code changed.** Produced `docs/audits/ISSUE_119_MINPARENTAGE_TRIAGE_2026-07-07.md` and posted a condensed version as a comment on #119. One `AskUserQuestion` (direction + whether to post); 0 stakeholder corrections.
- **Finding:** the conflict is real, already known, and was deliberately deferred. The package holds two independent notions of "minimum age to be a parent": **(A)** the legacy scalar `minParentAge` (sex/species-agnostic; default 2, but 3 in `getProductionStatus`) driving QC (`checkParentAge.R:94-95`), candidate-finding (`getPotentialParents.R:97`), and production status (`getProductionStatus.R:64`); and **(B)** a newer species+sex table (`speciesGestation$minMaleBreedingAge`/`minFemaleBreedingAge` via `getSpeciesMinBreedingAge`, e.g. rhesus male 4.0 / female 2.5) used by only the #9 GVA unknown-parent correction. A rhesus male sire at age 3 passes QC (3 ‚â• 2) but is below breeding age for (B) (floor 4); `getPotentialParents` is even internally inconsistent (species-aware gestation, flat-scalar breeding age).
- **Not a defect:** the plan that introduced (B) explicitly fenced this off ‚Äî `docs/planning/issue9-gva-unknown-parent-ranking-plan.md` ¬ß8-D (line 229): *"Do NOT retrofit `getPotentialParents`/`checkParentAge` ... Unifying breeding-age determination package-wide is a follow-up."* **#119 is that follow-up.**
- **Options laid out** (unify-on-table / sex-scalars-only / document-only / won't-fix); high blast radius (both QC sites sit under `qcStudbook`, `getPotentialParents` feeds breeding-group formation) ‚Üí any fix is vertical slices under strict TDD. **Owner decision (S301):** the resolution direction is **deferred to a dedicated planning session**; the triage recommends that planning session (settle Option + override precedence + unknown-species fallback + slice order) as the ratified next step. Not blocked (no missing data source, unlike #116).

### 2026-07-07 ‚Äî Remove the now-dead `fixGenotypeCols()` function (Session 300)
- **Deliverable (the tail of the S297/S299 #117 spinoff):** delete the orphaned `@noRd fixGenotypeCols()` function (`R/fixGenotypeCols.R`) + its test (`tests/testthat/test_fixGenotypeCols.R`) + its two dangling `inst/_pkgdown.yml` reference-index entries. **REFACTOR-only (dead-code deletion, S298 grep-branch: nothing executed in-package changes); one `AskUserQuestion` PRE-RED‚ÜíREFACTOR gate, one landing gate; 0 stakeholder corrections.** Landed as `374d08c5` on master (owner chose direct-commit); this docs close-out records that hash.
- **Why removable:** `fixGenotypeCols()` was a self-admitted band-aid ‚Äî its `@noRd` note said "This is not a good fix. A better solution is to avoid the problem. Currently qcStudbook() blindly changes all of the column names by removing the underscores." It repaired the genotype headers `first_name`/`second_name` that `fixColumnNames()`'s blanket underscore strip (`R/fixColumnNames.R:28`) collapsed to `firstname`/`secondname` ‚Äî actively producing the *un-documented* spelling of columns that `data.R`, `getPossibleCols.R`, `checkGenotypeFile.R`, `geneDrop.R`, and `headerDisplayNames.R` all document canonically *with* underscores. S297 (`fdcfc042`, #117) fixed that at the source (restore on the returned `newCols`), and S299 (`436dc749`) removed the redundant `fixGenotypeCols(sb)` call from `qcStudbook()`. After S299 the function had **zero in-package callers** (grep-confirmed across `R/`, `tests/`, `man/`, `NAMESPACE`, `inst/`, `vignettes/`) ‚Äî only its own test exercised it. Dead code kept alive by its own test.
- **Evidence-based inventory (mandatory for deletions):** `git grep fixGenotypeCols` over code paths returned only the definition, its own test, and two explanatory comments in `test_qcStudbook.R:340-342` (left intact ‚Äî they stay accurate and explain the S299 guard). It also surfaced two `inst/_pkgdown.yml` entries (lines 60, 156) that S299's notes did not mention ‚Äî dangling references, since a `@noRd` topic has no man page for pkgdown to link; removing the function makes removing them mandatory.
- **Coverage not lost:** the `first_name`/`second_name` invariant is covered end-to-end by the S299 guard test in `test_qcStudbook.R` (5 header spellings through `qcStudbook()`), and `fixColumnNames()` keeps its own `test_fixColumnNames.R`. `test_fixGenotypeCols.R` only exercised the deleted function.
- **Verify (firsthand):** full suite (`NOT_CRAN=true`) **0 failed / 0 error** (7 baseline warnings ‚Äî 2 `test_gvaConvergence_kinshipOverrides` + 5 `test_modPyramid` ‚Äî unchanged, none new); `document()` **zero `man/`/`NAMESPACE` delta** (the fn was `@noRd`, no man page ‚Üí deletion is doc-inert); `spell_check_package` clean; **`R CMD check --as-cran` Status: OK ‚Äî 0/0/0** (repo root, WITH vignettes; 3m12s). **Phase-3E covered by `--as-cran`** (it rebuilt vignettes and ran examples incl. `qcStudbook()` with the function gone ‚Äî Status OK is the runtime proof no path referenced it); no app launch needed (the deletion removed no runtime behavior ‚Äî the function already had no caller after S299). `lintr` **N/A** ‚Äî deletions only; no R source lines added or modified (the one edited file, `_pkgdown.yml`, is YAML).

### 2026-07-06 ‚Äî Remove the redundant `fixGenotypeCols()` call from `qcStudbook()` (Session 299)
- **Deliverable (owner picked the OTHER S297 spinoff):** remove the redundant `sb <- fixGenotypeCols(sb)` call at `R/qcStudbook.R:288`. **REFACTOR (behavior-inert removal of executed-but-inert code, S295/S298 precedent); one `AskUserQuestion` PRE-RED‚ÜíREFACTOR gate, one landing gate; 0 stakeholder corrections.** Landed as `436dc749` on master (owner chose direct-commit); this docs close-out records that hash.
- **Why redundant:** S297's `fixColumnNames()` fix (`fdcfc042`, #117) now restores every spelling of the genotype headers ‚Äî `first_name`/`second_name`, the space form (`First Name`), the period form (`first.name`), the already-collapsed form (`firstname`), and upper-case (`FIRSTNAME`) ‚Äî to canonical `first_name`/`second_name` *before* line 288. `fixGenotypeCols()` only renames a column literally named `firstname`/`secondname`, and no such column ever reaches line 288, so the call was a provable no-op. Its `@noRd` note already said "a better solution is to avoid the problem" ‚Äî S297's fix IS that better solution.
- **Behavior-inertness PROVEN before the edit (not reasoned):** an identity-override `identical()` battery (replacing `fixGenotypeCols` with `function(ped) ped` is exactly equivalent to removing the call) over 10 inputs (7 genotype-header spellings + the shipped 31-row `obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_breeder_genotypes.csv` fixture + `examplePedigree` + `pedGood`) returned `identical()==TRUE` under `reportErrors=FALSE/TRUE` and `reportChanges=TRUE`; a trace showed `fixGenotypeCols` **fires 0 times** at line 288 (never sees a bare `firstname`/`secondname`). An adversarial refutation workflow (4 independent agents running real R across exotic spellings, the reintroduction paths through `addUIds`/`addParents`/`removeDuplicates`, the `reportErrors=TRUE` and missing-column branches, real shipped fixtures, and duplicate/collision inputs) **could not refute it (0/4)**; one agent confirmed the proof is non-vacuous (with `fixGenotypeCols` forced to identity, a `firstname` input still emerges as `first_name` ‚Äî `fixColumnNames()` does the work).
- **Added a durable end-to-end guard test** to `tests/testthat/test_qcStudbook.R`: `qcStudbook()` on an `id/sire/dam/sex/birth + first_name/second_name` input keeps `first_name`/`second_name` and never `firstname`/`secondname`, across 5 header spellings. It passed WITH the call present (locking the invariant), then stayed green after removal ‚Äî so a future `fixColumnNames()` regression is caught now that the `fixGenotypeCols()` safety net is gone.
- **Scope kept surgical (FM #8):** the **call only**. The now-only-tested `@noRd fixGenotypeCols()` function + its `test_fixGenotypeCols.R` are left intact for a separate session's own strict-TDD evaluation.
- **Verify:** full suite (`NOT_CRAN=true`) **0 failed / 0 error** (3733 tests; 7 baseline warnings ‚Äî 2 `test_gvaConvergence_kinshipOverrides` + 5 `test_modPyramid` ‚Äî unchanged, none new); `lintr` 0 on both changed files; `spell_check_package` clean; `document()` **zero `man/`/`NAMESPACE` delta** (call removal is roxygen-inert); **`R CMD check --as-cran` Status: OK ‚Äî 0/0/0** (repo root, WITH vignettes). **Phase-3E covered by `--as-cran`** (examples + vignettes + full suite exercise `qcStudbook` with the call removed) plus the new guard test and the `test_modInput_qcStudbook.R` genotype-merge integration test.

Both symbols are used only as fully-qualified `lubridate::month`/`lubridate::day` (`getPyramidPlot.R:99,101`) ‚Äî grep confirms no bare `month()`/`day()` call exists in `R/` ‚Äî so the two imports were dead weight (behavior-inert to remove).

Landed as `aff4d262` on master (owner chose direct-commit).

### 2026-07-06 ‚Äî #117 ‚Äî fix `fixColumnNames()` first_name/second_name overreach cleanup so the restoration reaches newColNames (Session 297)
- **Deliverable:** fix the ineffective "clean up possible overreach" block in `R/fixColumnNames.R` (L31-36). The block restored the genotype-bearing headers `first_name`/`second_name` (which the underscore-strip pass at L28 collapses to `firstname`/`secondname`) but wrote to the local `cols`, which L39 (`cols <- newCols`) immediately overwrote; the function returns `newCols`, so the restoration never reached `newColNames`. Both the space form (`"First Name"`) and the underscore form (`"first_name"`) came back `firstname`/`secondname`. **Strict TDD bug fix; one `AskUserQuestion` PRE-RED‚ÜíRED gate (with the surgical-scope decision), one RED‚ÜíGREEN gate, one landing gate; 0 stakeholder corrections.**
- **Root cause + fix:** retarget the block from `cols` to `newCols` (the returned vector) ‚Äî `if (any(tolower(newCols) == "firstname"))` / `newCols[...] <- "first_name"` and the `secondname` twin. A minimal in-body change (2 condition + 2 assignment lines retargeted; the inline comment expanded to state the intent + issue). Now every spelling of the two genotype headers ‚Äî space (`First Name`), underscore (`first_name`), period (`First.Name`), and the already-collapsed form (`firstname`) ‚Äî normalizes to canonical `first_name`/`second_name` in `newColNames`. The block now mirrors the downstream `fixGenotypeCols()` workaround exactly.
- **RED ‚Üí GREEN:** rewrote `tests/testthat/test_fixColumnNames.R` from the 2 characterization tests (which pinned the *defective* output per the #111 coverage-slice note) to 8 blocks / 10 assertions asserting the *corrected* contract ‚Äî all four input spellings, only-first, only-second, mixed-with-canonical (`EGO`/`Sire_ID` + genotype), the `underScoreRemoved == character(0)` diagnostic for the underscore form (the previously spurious record is gone), plus a no-genotype invariant guard. RED: 7/8 blocks fail (9 assertion failures), the invariant guard passes. GREEN: all 10 assertions pass, invariant stays green.
- **Verify (firsthand):** `test_fixColumnNames.R` 8/8 pass; full-suite regression read (`NOT_CRAN=true`) **244 files, 0 failed / 0 error** (7 baseline warnings ‚Äî `test_gvaConvergence_kinshipOverrides` + `test_modPyramid`, none new); `lintr::lint()` on both changed files = **0**; `spell_check_package` clean; `devtools::document()` **zero `man/` delta** (the fix is inside the function body, roxygen-inert) ‚Äî reverted the known pre-existing `lubridate` day/month NAMESPACE drift `document()` re-surfaced; `R CMD check --as-cran` (repo root, WITH vignettes) **Status: OK ‚Äî 0/0/0**.
- **Phase-3E (integration):** `fixColumnNames()`'s only production caller is `qcStudbook()` (`:179`), which also runs the idempotent `fixGenotypeCols()` workaround (`:288`), so the fix leaves `qcStudbook`'s *net* output unchanged ‚Äî proven by `test_qcStudbook`/`test_species_first_class`/`test_exampleData_columnNames` all passing unchanged, and by a live `qcStudbook()` run on a genotype-bearing pedigree returning `first_name`/`second_name` with no collapsed forms. Scope kept surgical: `fixGenotypeCols` left as a harmless safety net for a separate future evaluation.

### 2026-07-06 ‚Äî #111 ‚Äî remove the last uncovered line (proven-dead branch in dataframe2string.R) ‚Üí package 100% coverage (Session 295)
- **Deliverable (owner directed the removal over a `# nocov` waive):** delete the genuinely-dead defensive branch `R/dataframe2string.R` L47-50 (`if (is.null(rowNames)) rowNames <- as.character(seq_len(NROW(m)))`). **REFACTOR-only (dead-code removal, no behavior change); one `AskUserQuestion` PRE-RED‚ÜíREFACTOR gate, one landing gate; 0 stakeholder corrections.**
- **Why it is dead:** for a data frame with ‚â•1 row, `dimnames(object)[[1L]]` (== `row.names()`) is never NULL; the 0-row case already exits at L36-37; a matrix either has 0 rows (‚Üí L37) or has row names (‚Üí the `is.null` guard is FALSE). So the `if` never fires for any input the function can process (S293 first proved this; S294 flagged it as a waive-or-remove decision).
- **Verify (firsthand):** **behavior-inert proof** ‚Äî `identical()` output between the current and guard-removed versions over 10 inputs (data frames with default + custom row names, 1-column, 1-row, 0-column, 0-row, matrices with and without row names, and `addRowNames=FALSE`) ‚Äî ALL identical; existing `test_dataframe2string.R` + full suite **0 failed / 0 error / 0 true offenders** (7 baseline warnings, none new); `covr` (`NOT_CRAN=true`) confirms `dataframe2string.R` **100%** and **overall package coverage 99.99% ‚Üí 100.00%**; `lintr::lint()` = **0**; `devtools::document()` produced **zero `man/` delta** (roxygen-inert); `R CMD check --as-cran` (repo root, WITH vignettes) **Status: OK ‚Äî 0/0/0**. Phase-3E N/A ‚Äî pure exported function, no runtime wiring (the only caller is `test_dataframe2string.R`). Landed as `dfedaa90` on master (owner chose direct-commit).
- **This brings the package to 100% line coverage package-wide, effectively completing issue #111.**
- **Separate finding (NOT fixed here ‚Äî outside scope):** the committed `NAMESPACE` carries two redundant lines, `importFrom(lubridate, day)` and `importFrom(lubridate, month)`, that no roxygen `@importFrom` declares (`getPyramidPlot.R` uses `lubridate::day`/`lubridate::month` via explicit `::`, needing no import). `document()` removes them, so it is currently non-idempotent for `NAMESPACE`. Harmless (checks 0/0/0 with them); a ~30-second cleanup for its own session.

### 2026-07-06 ‚Äî #111 code-coverage campaign, slice 10 ‚Äî the last two Shiny modules (modPedigree + modBreedingGroups) backfilled to 100% (Session 294)
- **Deliverable (owner said "continue #111", then approved batching both remaining modules):** the two remaining sub-100% Shiny modules driven to 100% line coverage via `shiny::testServer` backfill tests. **Test-only (no production code changed); one `AskUserQuestion` PRE-RED‚ÜíRED + scope gate, one landing gate; 0 stakeholder corrections.**
- **Files and the exact branch each test reached** (uncovered lines dumped with `covr::zero_coverage`, `NOT_CRAN=true`; each proven reachable by a `covr::function_coverage` probe before writing):
  - `modPedigree.R` 97.72% ‚Üí **100%** ‚Äî L274-277, the focal-file `read.csv` error handler (existing tests only upload valid CSVs); and L371/L374, the export `downloadHandler` filename + content (never invoked). New `test_modPedigree_coverage.R` (2 `testServer` tests): a `focalAnimalFile$datapath` pointing at a nonexistent path makes `read.csv` throw ‚Üí the `tryCatch` error branch runs, leaving the typed IDs intact; `path <- output$exportPedigree` runs both handler fns ‚Üí a real 5-row CSV.
  - `modBreedingGroups.R` 98.56% ‚Üí **100%** ‚Äî L175, the `getKinshipMatrix` branch that returns a geneticValues-supplied kinship matrix (every existing test passes `geneticValues=NULL`, forcing the pedigree recompute); L505/510/515, the NULL-`groupResults` guards in the returned `score`/`unassigned`/`groupKinship` reactives (existing tests always form groups first); and L269, the `currentGroups` truncation guard. New `test_modBreedingGroups_coverage.R` (3 `testServer` tests): `geneticValues=reactive(list(id, kinship=kmat))` ‚Üí L175 (proven via `groupResults()$kmat` identical to the supplied matrix); reading `score()`/`unassigned()`/`groupKinship()` before `formGroups` ‚Üí the three NULL guards at once; `nGroups=0` ‚Üí the truncation guard L269.
- **L269 was TESTED, not waived** (unlike `dataframe2string` L49, which S293 proved unreachable). The guard fires only when `currentGroups` (length 1 when unseeded) exceeds `numGp`, i.e. `numGp < 1`; the sole trigger is `nGroups=0` (below the UI `min=1`). The `function_coverage` probe proved it reachable without an uncaught error. The degenerate input also makes the downstream formation warn ("items to replace"), suppressed at the test since the guard itself is clean.
- **Verify (firsthand):** both new files 5 tests / 0 failed / 0 error / 0 warning; `covr` (full suite, `NOT_CRAN=true`) confirms both files **100%** and overall **99.83% ‚Üí 99.99%**; full-suite regression read **0 failed / 0 error / 0 true offenders** (7 baseline warnings ‚Äî `test_gvaConvergence_kinshipOverrides` + `test_modPyramid`, none from the new files); `lintr::lint()` on both new files = **0**; `R CMD check --as-cran` (repo root, WITH vignettes) **Status: OK ‚Äî 0/0/0**. Phase-3E N/A ‚Äî test-only, no `R/` or runtime change (FM #24 has no target). Landed as `2613af12` on master (owner chose direct-commit).
- **This closes the last two sub-100% Shiny modules.** The only file now below 100% package-wide is `dataframe2string.R` (96.88%, the proven-unreachable L49 ‚Äî a `# nocov` waive decision, a separate 1-line production choice).

### 2026-07-06 ‚Äî #111 code-coverage campaign, slice 9 ‚Äî six single-file helpers backfilled to 100% in one batched session (Session 293)
- **Deliverable (owner said "continue #111 with checkKinshipOverrides", then approved batching the remaining trivial single-file residuals into one session):** the first post-Shiny-module tier ‚Äî six plain-function helper files driven to 100% line coverage via ordinary `test_that` backfill tests. **Test-only (no production code changed); three `AskUserQuestion` gates ‚Äî a PRE-RED‚ÜíRED for `checkKinshipOverrides`, then a batch-scope decision, then a combined PRE-RED‚ÜíRED for the other five; 0 stakeholder corrections.**
- **Files and the exact branch each test reached** (uncovered lines dumped with `covr::zero_coverage`, `NOT_CRAN=true`, before writing):
  - `checkKinshipOverrides.R` 96.43% ‚Üí **100%** ‚Äî L47, the non-numeric `kinship`-column `stop()` (existing tests only pass numeric / NA / negative kinship). New test: a character and a factor kinship column ‚Üí `expect_error(..., "must be numeric")`.
  - `correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R` 97.30% ‚Üí **100%** ‚Äî L146 (unnamed `indivMeanKin` ‚Üí early return) and L152 (a ped with no `exit` column ‚Üí `exit` defaulted to `as.Date(NA)`). Two `test_that` cases on separate branches.
  - `loadSpeciesOverrides.R` 96.72% ‚Üí **100%** ‚Äî L139‚Äì140, the internal `mergeSpeciesOverrides` append branch for a species absent from the bundled table (every existing test overrides "RHESUS", already present). New test: append a novel "GORILLA" row.
  - `orderReport.R` 97.73% ‚Üí **100%** ‚Äî L42, the `getFounders(ped)` fallback taken when the report has no `parentage` column.
  - `getPyramidPlot.R` 98.36% ‚Üí **100%** ‚Äî L40, the `ageUnit == "months"` age-conversion branch.
  - `getPotentialParents.R` 96.88% ‚Üí **100%** ‚Äî L151‚Äì152, the empty-`potentialDams` fallback to all old-enough females (a candidate dam with no offspring is dropped by the proven-breeder filter, emptying the set and forcing the fallback, which re-admits her).
- **`dataframe2string.R` L49 was investigated and PROVEN unreachable for the documented input type, and left untouched** (this was NOT a production change): `dimnames.data.frame(x)` is `list(row.names(x), names(x))`, so `dimnames(object)[[1L]]` mirrors `row.names()` and is NULL only when `nRows == 0`, which is already intercepted upstream at L36‚Äì37; a matrix cannot reach it either. It is inherited defensive code from `print.data.frame`. Waiving it (or a `# nocov`) is a separate future decision, not a coverage-backfill task. The file stays at 96.88%.
- **Method:** the six single-file investigations were fanned out to parallel probe agents (a `Workflow`), each of which PROVED its fixture reaches the target line with `covr::function_coverage(fn, code)` before any repo file was touched; the one unreachable line was caught and dropped by that proof step.
- **Result:** all six target files at **100%**; overall coverage 99.71% ‚Üí **99.83%** (`NOT_CRAN=true`). Only three files remain sub-100%: `dataframe2string` (the unreachable defensive line) and the two deferred Shiny modules `modPedigree` (97.72%) and `modBreedingGroups` (98.56%), which need `testServer` slices.
- **Verified:** the 6 modified test files run 0 failed / 0 error / 0 warning; `covr` (full suite, `NOT_CRAN=true`) confirms all six at 100% and overall 99.83%; full-suite regression read 0 failed / 0 error / 0 true offenders (7 baseline warnings, none from the changed files); `lintr::lint()` on all six changed files = 0; `R CMD check --as-cran` (repo root, WITH vignettes, via `devtools::check`) Status: OK ‚Äî 0 errors / 0 warnings / 0 notes. Phase-3E N/A (test-only, no runtime surface).

The whole server body ‚Äî the three output renderers (`siteInfo`, `colonySummary`, `geneticDiversity`), both `downloadHandler`s, and the returned `colonySummary` reactive ‚Äî ran only under the opt-in browser e2e (`test-e2e-orip-module.R`), which skips without shinytest2 + chromote. That is why the file read 31.7% while looking "tested".
- **New file `tests/testthat/test_modORIPReporting_server.R` ‚Äî 12 `testServer` tests:** siteInfo (populated config table / `getSiteInfo` NULL-fallback / `tryCatch`‚Üí"not available" on error); colonySummary (populated counts / empty "No data"); geneticDiversity (populated metrics / empty "Run genetic value analysis" prompt); `downloadORIPReport` (full site+colony+diversity CSV / NULL-ped+gv, which also hits the `getSiteInfo` else-branch); `downloadDemographics` (pedigree CSV / placeholder); returned `colonySummary()` reactive. renderUI/renderTable read via `any(grepl(..., as.character(output$x)))` (note `as.character` of a rendered UI yields a length-2 vector ‚Äî html + `"list()"` deps), downloadHandlers via `path <- output$downloadX; read.csv(path)`. Fixtures validated with a throwaway `load_all`/`testServer` probe before the file was written.
- **Result:** `R/modORIPReporting.R` **31.7% ‚Üí 100%** (zero uncovered lines); overall coverage **92.96% ‚Üí 95.03%** (`NOT_CRAN=true`).
- **Verify (firsthand):** new file **12/12** green; `covr` re-measure confirms `modORIPReporting.R` at 100% under the full suite; **full suite (`NOT_CRAN=true`) 1449 tests, 0 failed / 0 error, 0 true offenders**; **lint** new file 0 (`.lintr` excludes `tests/`, and no `R/` source changed ‚Üí non-regressing); `spell_check_package` **clean**; **`R CMD check --as-cran` (repo root, WITH vignettes) Status: OK ‚Äî 0 errors / 0 warnings / 0 notes** (cleaner than the documented 0/0/2 baseline; the CRAN-incoming "archived/New submission" + HTML-Tidy NOTEs do not fire under `devtools::check`). **Phase-3E N/A:** test-only, no `R/` source changed, no runtime behavior ‚Äî no runtime surface to smoke-test (not a skipped check).
- **Campaign status:** #111 stays OPEN. Slices done: S1 helper tier (S285), **S2 `modORIPReporting` server (this session)**. Remaining genuine gaps for future slices: `appServer` (0% local ‚Äî needs a full-app `testServer` harness) and small residuals (`modInput` 87.3%, `modPyramid` 89.7%, `modSummaryStats` 94.3%, `modPotentialParents` 94.7%, `modGeneticValue` 96.3%).

### 2026-07-06 ‚Äî #111 code-coverage campaign, slice 1 ‚Äî deterministic helper tier backfilled to 100% + dead-code removal (Session 285)
- **Deliverable (owner picked #111, then clarified scope = increase coverage across *all* R code lacking quality coverage, the three named examples being illustrative; owner then chose this session's slice via `AskUserQuestion`: the "deterministic helper tier"):** add test-backfill for the genuinely-untested helper functions and remove one dead file. **Test-only (no production logic changed) + one dead-code deletion; PRE-RED‚ÜíRED gate via `AskUserQuestion`; 0 stakeholder corrections.**
- **Measurement correction (load-bearing):** an initial `covr::package_coverage()` read 83.54%, but that *undercounts* ‚Äî 39 test files carry `testthat::skip_on_cran()` (the module `testServer` suites) and covr does not set `NOT_CRAN`, so those tests were skipped. Re-measured with `NOT_CRAN=true`: **true baseline 91.23%**. This corrected the target list: `set_seed.R` and `applyKinshipOverridesToMatrix.R` (flagged low by the artifact read) are actually already 100% under `NOT_CRAN=true` and were dropped from scope, and the "low" Shiny modules (`modBreedingGroups` 98.6%, `modGeneticValue` 96.3%, `modInput` 87.3%) are mostly the same artifact, not genuine gaps.
- **16 helper files backfilled to 100%** (each targeting its exact uncovered lines ‚Äî mostly error/guard/format/fallback branches no prior fixture reached): `logModuleEvent`, `safeExecute`, `savePlotToFile`, `summarizeKinshipValues`, `makeGeneticSummaryTable`, `shouldShowChangedColsTab`, `fixColumnNames`, `getConfigApiKey`, `hasNetrc`, `readKinshipOverrides`, `getGenoDefinedParentGenotypes`, `saveDataframesAsFiles`, `makeExamplePedigreeFile`, `gvaConvergence`, `runQcStudbook`, `getLkDirectAncestors`. Six new `test_*.R` files, nine appended. Mocking used where a branch needs it: `testthat::local_mocked_bindings` (mock `qcStudbook`/`create_wkbk`/`shiny::showNotification`), `mockery::stub` (mock `getDemographics` for the LabKey walk), `withr` for option/env/tempfile isolation, and a null plot device.
- **Dead-code removal:** deleted `R/agePyramidPlot.R` ‚Äî an unexported (`@noRd`), **zero-caller** duplicate of the live `getPyramidPlot()` (verified no references in `R/`, `tests/`, `NAMESPACE`, or `man/`; committed since `fb9a92ab`); removed its two stale `inst/_pkgdown.yml` reference-index entries (it has no man page, so pkgdown referenced a non-existent topic). No `NAMESPACE` change (it was internal).
- **Bug found, filed, not fixed (scope discipline):** while writing the `fixColumnNames` test, found that its "clean up possible overreach" block (lines 30‚Äì36) is **ineffective** ‚Äî it restores `first_name`/`second_name` into the local `cols`, but line 39 (`cols <- newCols`) overwrites it and the function returns `newCols`, so the restoration never reaches `newColNames` (both `"First Name"` and `"first_name"` inputs collapse to `"firstname"`). Filed as **#117** (`bug`); the coverage test **characterizes the current (defective) behavior** (clearly commented, pointing at #117) rather than fixing it in a coverage slice.
- **Result:** overall coverage **91.23% ‚Üí 92.96%** (`NOT_CRAN=true`); all 16 target files at 100%.
- **Verify (firsthand):** each new/changed test file run green individually; **full suite (`NOT_CRAN=true`) 1437 tests, 0 failed / 0 error, 0 true offenders** (the `test-app-*`/`test-e2e-*` baseline excluded); `covr` re-measure confirms every target file at 100%; **lint non-regressing** (`.lintr` excludes `tests/`; no `R/` source was added or modified ‚Äî only a deletion; new files lint 0); `spell_check_package` **clean**; **`R CMD check --as-cran` (repo root) GREEN ‚Äî 0 errors / 0 warnings / 2 benign NOTEs** (archived "New submission" + HTML-Tidy) with `testthat.R OK`. **Phase-3E N/A:** no runtime behavior changed ‚Äî the deletion removes a zero-caller function (verified) and the rest is test-only, so there is no runtime surface to smoke-test (not a skipped check).
- **Campaign status:** #111 stays OPEN. This is slice 1 (helper tier). Remaining genuine gaps for future slices: `modORIPReporting` (31.7%, genuinely uncovered ‚Äî no `testServer` tests) and `appServer` (0% local ‚Äî needs a full-app `testServer` harness).
